---
title: "NLP_draft1"
author: "Daniel"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Packages

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(haven)
library(tidytext)
library(rvest)
library(janitor)
library(xml2)
library(topicmodels)
library(stm)
library(reshape2)
```

## Loading Data

```{r}
speech_sample_5 <- read.csv("data/Carney 5 speech sample.csv")
```

## Top Word Frequency from First 5 Speeches from PM Website - manual scraping

```{r}
# Tokenizing and stopping
word_counts_sample <- speech_sample_5 |>
  unnest_tokens(word, text) |>
  anti_join(stop_words, by = "word") |>
  count(word, sort = TRUE)

top_words_sample <- word_counts_sample |>
  slice_max(n, n = 20)

top_words_sample |>
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(word, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Most Frequent Words in Carney First 5 Speech Sample",
    x = "Word",
    y = "Frequency"
  )
```

## Scraping

```{r}
speech_urls <- read.csv("Data/carney_speech_urls.csv")

scrape_pm_speech <- function(url) {
  
  page <- read_html(url)
  
  lines <- page |>
    html_text2() |>
    str_split("\\n") |>
    pluck(1) |>
    str_squish()
  
  lines <- lines[lines != ""]
  
  # Title appears before "Type keyword(s)" and after navigation
  title_index <- which(str_detect(lines, "Prime Minister Carney"))[1]
  title <- lines[title_index]
  
  # Date and location are on the same line, e.g. "June 9, 2025 Toronto, Ontario"
  date_index <- which(str_detect(
    lines,
    "^[A-Z][a-z]+ \\d{1,2}, \\d{4} "
  ))[1]
  
  if (is.na(date_index)) {
    warning(str_c("No date found for: ", url))
    return(tibble(
      title = title,
      source = "Prime Minister of Canada",
      source_type = "speech",
      text = NA_character_
    ))
  }
  
  # Speech starts immediately after the date/location line
  start_index <- date_index + 1
  
  # Find where speech ends
  footer_index <- which(str_detect(
    lines,
    "\\*\\s*\\*\\s*\\*|Footer Menus|Privacy Statement|Social media|Share this Page"
  ))
  
  # Only use footer markers that appear AFTER the speech starts
  footer_index <- footer_index[footer_index > start_index][1]
  
  if (is.na(footer_index)) {
    end_index <- length(lines)
  } else {
    end_index <- footer_index - 1
  }
  
  speech_text <- lines[start_index:end_index] |>
    str_c(collapse = " ") |>
    str_squish()
  
   tibble(
    title = title,
    source = "Prime Minister of Canada",
    source_type = "speech",
    text = speech_text
  )
}

```

```{r}
carney_speeches_raw <- speech_urls |>
  mutate(data = map(url, scrape_pm_speech)) |>
  tidyr::unnest(cols = c(data))

carney_speeches <- carney_speeches_raw |>
  mutate(
    date = str_extract(url, "\\d{4}/\\d{2}/\\d{2}"),
    date = ymd(str_replace_all(date, "/", "-"))
  ) |>
  select(date, title, url, source, source_type, text)

write_csv(carney_speeches, "data/carney_pm_speeches_raw.csv")
```

```{r}
word_counts <- carney_speeches |>
  unnest_tokens(word, text) |>
  anti_join(stop_words, by = "word") |>
  count(word, sort = TRUE)

top_words <- word_counts |>
  slice_max(n, n = 50)
top_words

```

# Lemmatization (grouping words based on base form)

```{r}
word_counts_grouped_by_speech <- carney_speeches |>
  mutate(doc_id = row_number()) |>
  unnest_tokens(word, text) |>
  mutate(word = str_replace(word, "’s$|'s$", "")) |>
  anti_join(stop_words, by = "word") |>
  mutate(word = case_when(
    word %in% c("canadian", "canadians") ~ "canada",
    word %in% c("building", "built") ~ "build",
    word %in% c("investments") ~ "investment",
    word %in% c("partnerships", "partners") ~ "partnership",
    word %in% c("projects") ~ "project",
    word %in% c("stronger", "strength") ~ "strong",
    word %in% c("nations", "national") ~ "nation",
    word %in% c("economic") ~ "economy",
    word %in% c("creating") ~ "create",
    word %in% c("homes", "housing") ~ "home",
    TRUE ~ word
  )) |>
count(doc_id, word, sort = TRUE)

speech_dtm <- word_counts_grouped_by_speech |>
  cast_dtm(document = doc_id, term = word, value = n)

word_counts_grouped_by_speech
```

## Fitting model

```{r}
carney_speeches_lda <- LDA(speech_dtm, k = 4, control = list(seed = 712))

```

## 
```{r}
carney_topics <- tidy(carney_speeches_lda, matrix = "beta")
carney_topics
```

```{r}
carney_top_topics <- carney_topics |>
  group_by(topic)|>
  slice_max(beta, n=10)|>
  ungroup() |>
  arrange(topic, -beta)

carney_top_topics %>%
  mutate(term = reorder_within(term, beta, topic)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(beta, term, fill = factor(topic))) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ topic, scales = "free") +
  scale_y_reordered()

```

## Notes
-   "Canada" and "Build" dominate every topic
